_draft_note: INERT STAGING. Lives under godot/data/_drafts/, never loaded by dialogue_runner. Promote to godot/data/dialogues/mail_carrier_ch1.json once Piotr authors the lines. Mail carrier is a transactional street NPC on office_street.tscn (sidewalk middle band). The 2026-05-18 sprint deferred a patrol behaviour — for now the carrier is static and the dialogue covers a single delivery-themed exchange. Per the transactional carve-out in godot/AGENTS.md §First-meeting introductions, no name-exchange first-meeting state is required, but the carrier may legitimately know the firm name from his round and acknowledge it.
_chapter_ref: Chapter 1 ambient. Beat 1-2 (arrival window) but reusable anywhere in Ch1. No quest-critical content.
_address_form_note: Toward Cula: 'Dr. A. Cula' if direct address is used. The carrier likely reads the firm signage (Pig & Swine) before he reads the man, so opening 'Pig & Swine?' or 'For Pig & Swine?' is on-register. Avoid 'Pan mecenasie' / 'Pan doktorze' — English-first per godot/AGENTS.md.
_authoring_todo: Replace each '_doc:' placeholder with the finished line.
Decide whether mail_carrier_first_approach should hand the player a diegetic envelope (would require an inventory mutation in on_dismiss + a pickup item — out of scope for the skeleton).
Decide whether the carrier reacts to Beat 13 (firm-survives) with a different repeat line; if yes, gate a third state on chapter1.case_won.

lines: _doc: DRAFT — one line. Carrier checks his ledger or his sack, names the firm out loud ('Pig & Swine?'), no envelope handed over in this skeleton.
_doc: DRAFT — one line, repeatable. The route continues. A second observation about the round or the weight of the sack; nothing that advances quest state.
